headline: The Five Eyes alliance warned that AI's threat to cybersecurity now arrives in months as Anthropic's Mythos model found flaws in classified US systems and its safety-tuned Fable 5 fell to jailbreaks within days.

emerging_trends.text: Western intelligence agencies in the Five Eyes alliance warned that AI's offensive potential is measured in months while Anthropic's Mythos model surfaced flaws in classified US government systems, signaling that frontier models now act as cyber weapons.
emerging_trends.topic_title: AI Models Cross Into Offensive Cyber Operations

emerging_trends.text: Attackers keep smuggling hostile code into trusted software through malicious pull requests and fake agent skills, eroding the assumption that vetted marketplaces and CI/CD pipelines are safe.
emerging_trends.topic_title: Malicious Code Slips Into Developer and Agent Pipelines

emerging_trends.text: Law enforcement landed back-to-back wins as two Scattered Spider members admitted crippling Transport for London and the Justice Department seized scam-laundering infrastructure tied to Cambodia's HuiOne Group.
emerging_trends.topic_title: Scattered Spider Guilty Pleas Over Transport for London

topics.title: AI Models Cross Into Offensive Cyber Operations
topics.latest_developments: The Five Eyes intelligence alliance issued a joint alert warning that AI's cybersecurity threat arrives in months rather than years, the same day a US official said Anthropic's Mythos model found vulnerabilities in classified government systems within hours and researchers bypassed the guardrails on the safety-tuned Fable 5 within days.
topics.summary: Frontier AI models now discover software flaws faster than defenders can respond, and jailbreaks strip safeguards almost as quickly as vendors ship them. OpenAI refocused its Daybreak initiative on patching over discovery as officials warned both offensive capability and circumvention are accelerating.

sources.title: Five Eyes agencies sound alarm about AI's threat to cybersecurity
Anthropic's Mythos Model Found Vulnerabilities in Classified US Government Systems, Official Says
Anthropic's Fable 5 Model Jailbroken Within Days
OpenAI Refocuses Cybersecurity Efforts on Patching Over Discovery

topics.title: DoJ Seizes HuiOne Scam-Finance Infrastructure
topics.latest_developments: The Justice Department seized a cloud computing account that subsidiaries of Cambodia's HuiOne Group used to move cyber-scam proceeds as the Treasury sanctioned nine individuals and 26 entities tied to Prince Group, and prosecutors extradited Algerian national Abdellah Belmili to face charges of running the Market0Day and Spoxy cybercrime marketplaces.
topics.summary: HuiOne, cut off from the US financial system last year, allegedly helped launder money from Southeast Asian scam compounds. Belmili, 26, faces up to 30 years in prison.

sources.title: DoJ Seizes Huione Cloud Account Tied to Cyber Scam Money Laundering
Feds seize alleged cyber-scam infrastructure connected to Southeast Asian company
Algerian Man Extradited to US for Running Cybercrime Marketplaces

topics.title: Cisco, Ubiquiti, and Lantronix Flaws Under Active Attack
topics.latest_developments: BleepingComputer, SecurityWeek, and The Hacker News confirmed attackers now exploit Cisco Unified Communications Manager flaw CVE-2026-20230 after a proof of concept exposed a file-write path to root, and CISA added four actively exploited bugs to its catalog—three in Ubiquiti UniFi OS and the Lantronix EDS5000 code-injection flaw CVE-2025-67038.
topics.summary: CVE-2026-20230 is a server-side request forgery flaw in Cisco Unified CM that lets unauthenticated attackers reach internal systems. Administrators should apply Cisco's June patches alongside the Ubiquiti UniFi OS and Lantronix fixes without delay.

sources.title: Cisco Unified CM Flaw Exploited After PoC Reveals File-Write Path to Root
Cisco Unified CM flaw CVE-2026-20230 now exploited in attacks
CISA Adds Four Known Exploited Vulnerabilities to Catalog

topics.title: Scattered Spider Guilty Pleas Over Transport for London
topics.latest_developments: Two men, aged 20 and 18, pleaded guilty in the United Kingdom on the first day of their trial to the August 2024 cyberattack that crippled Transport for London for months.
topics.summary: The pair belonged to Scattered Spider, the cybercrime group behind a string of major intrusions across retail, telecom, and transit. Their pleas ended a case expected to run six weeks and mark a rare conviction of the group's members.

sources.title: Scattered Spider Hackers Plead Guilty on Day 1 of Trial
Scattered Spider members plead guilty to hacking Transport for London
Two Scattered Spider members plead guilty over cyberattack that crippled London transit

topics.title: Malicious Code Slips Into Developer and Agent Pipelines
topics.latest_developments: Dark Reading detailed Cordyceps, a malicious pull-request technique that abuses CI/CD weaknesses in Microsoft's Azure Sentinel, Google's AI Agent Development Kit, Apache's Doris database, Cloudflare's Workers SDK, and Python's Black, while GitHub updated actions/checkout to block such pwn-request attacks and security firm AIR slipped a fake AI agent skill past every scanner it tested to reach roughly 26,000 agents.
topics.summary: Attackers increasingly plant hostile code in trusted developer pipelines and agent marketplaces, where automated scans wave it through. Teams should pin and review third-party actions, restrict pull_request_target triggers, and vet agent skills before deployment.

sources.title: 'Cordyceps': Mushrooming Malicious Pull Requests Threaten Developer Workflows
GitHub Updates actions/checkout to Block Common Pwn Request Attack Patterns
Fake AI Agent Skill Passed Security Scans and Reportedly Reached 26,000 Agents

topics.title: Federal Post-Quantum Migration Deadlines Set
topics.latest_developments: Coverage of executive order EO 14409 detailed its concrete deadlines—federal agencies must move key establishment to post-quantum cryptography by December 31, 2030, and digital signatures by December 31, 2031—while leaving national security systems on a separate track.
topics.summary: The order, signed June 22, 2026, accelerates the government's shift to encryption that resists future quantum computers, citing harvest-now-decrypt-later risk. Agencies must inventory high-value assets and high-impact systems first.

sources.title: Trump Order Sets 2030 Deadline for Federal Post-Quantum Crypto Migration
White House drastically shortens deadline for dropping quantum-vulnerable crypto
Trump directs federal agencies to protect US data from quantum threats

weather: Thursday: Partly Sunny then Chance Showers And Thunderstorms, high 84F.

games.recap: Cole Young hits a two-run home run in the seventh to lift Mariners to a 3-2 victory over Pirates

business_politics.title: Venezuela Readies Record $240 Billion Debt Restructuring
business_politics.latest_developments: Caracas will disclose roughly $240 billion in borrowing, far above prior estimates, launching the largest sovereign debt restructuring in history.
business_politics.summary: Venezuela, following the overthrow of Nicolás Maduro, prepares to reveal a $240 billion debt pile and seek re-entry into global capital markets, a workout that would surpass any sovereign restructuring on record.

sources.title: Venezuela to reveal $240bn debt pile in world’s largest restructuring
Venezuela faces world’s largest debt restructuring

business_politics.title: Dollar Hits 13-Month High as Markets Price a Fed Hike
business_politics.latest_developments: After the two days of tech-led losses reported yesterday, equities steadied while the DXY dollar index climbed to a 13-month high and rates markets began pricing a possible Federal Reserve rate increase this year.
business_politics.summary: A flight to safety lifted the dollar to its strongest in 13 months and drove the euro to a one-year low as investors who had bet on Fed cuts shifted toward the chance of a 2026 hike, pressuring gold, lifting Treasury yields, and stabilizing Wall Street after the AI-stock selloff.

sources.title: Stock Market Today: Markets Steady After Two-Day Tech Slide
Dollar’s Outperformance Could Continue if Risk Aversion Persists
U.S. Treasury Yields Edge Higher

business_politics.title: Oil Eases as Hormuz Shipping Resumes
business_politics.latest_developments: Crude fell as shipping through the Strait of Hormuz gradually resumed and international authorities reported improved safety, easing the blockade reported earlier this week.
business_politics.summary: Allianz still counts roughly $125 billion of vessels and cargo stranded in the Persian Gulf after the Strait of Hormuz closure and reported mining, while oil retreated Wednesday as traffic began moving again and President Trump called for a probe into why U.S. gasoline prices stayed high as crude fell.

sources.title: Oil Falls on Hormuz Activity Optimism
Around $125 Billion of Vessels, Cargo Remain Stranded in Persian Gulf, Allianz Says
Trump Calls for Probe Into U.S. Gasoline Prices After Oil Falls

business.title: Pennsylvania House Passes $1.7 Billion Electricity Tax Cut
business.latest_developments: The Pennsylvania House passed a $1.7 billion electricity tax cut and a measure setting a formula for utilities' 'reasonable return' on equity when they pursue rate increases.
business.summary: Pennsylvania's House approved legislation lowering electricity taxes by $1.7 billion and capping utility profits by defining a reasonable return on equity for rate hikes, aiming to ease power bills statewide.

sources.title: Pa. House passes $1.7B tax cut on electricity, reins in utility company profits

business.title: Pittsburgh Region Keeps Losing Residents
business.latest_developments: A Pittsburgh City Paper analysis reports that most communities across the region have shed residents since the 2020 census and argues the loss need not signal decline.
business.summary: Pittsburgh City Paper finds that most municipalities in the Pittsburgh region have lost population since 2020 and challenges the planning orthodoxy equating growth with success, contending stable or shrinking populations can still mark healthy places.

sources.title: Most Pittsburgh‑area communities are losing residents — here’s why that might be OK

around_town.title: Commercial Street Closes Thursday for Bridge Work
around_town.latest_developments: PennDOT confirmed Commercial Street will close from 6 a.m. to 4 p.m. Thursday, June 25, and will also shut the Nine Mile Run Trail to bicyclists and pedestrians that day.
around_town.summary: PennDOT will close Commercial Street where it runs beneath the Parkway East outside the Squirrel Hill Tunnel for one day of testing ahead of next month's bridge-replacement project near Frick Park.

sources.title: Commercial Street closing Thursday for Parkway East bridge replacement testing work

around_town.title: Pittsburgh Officer Charged With Stealing Time From Target Job
around_town.latest_developments: Authorities charged a Pittsburgh police officer Tuesday with misdemeanor theft by deception for 'stealing time' from his secondary job at the Target store in East Liberty.
around_town.summary: A Pittsburgh police officer faces a misdemeanor theft-by-deception charge after authorities say he billed hours he did not work at his secondary job at the Target in East Liberty.

sources.title: Pittsburgh cop with side gig at Target charged with ‘stealing time’

around_town.title: Chartiers Valley Outsources Its Bus Service
around_town.latest_developments: The Chartiers Valley School Board narrowly voted Tuesday night to hand busing to a private provider and furlough 41 drivers, over objections from roughly 90 people at the meeting.
around_town.summary: Chartiers Valley School Board members approved a contract turning student transportation over to a private company and furloughing 41 district drivers, a decision that drew emotional protest from parents and bus drivers.

sources.title: Chartiers Valley school board votes to outsource transportation; 41 drivers to be furloughed
Chartiers Valley outsources transportation services in heated meeting

events.title: Eddie Ifft Plays the Oaks Theater
events.latest_developments: Comedian Eddie Ifft, a Fox Chapel native, headlines the Oaks Theater in Oakmont on Thursday, July 2, 2026.
events.summary: Eddie Ifft, the Pittsburgh-born stand-up comedian who grew up in Fox Chapel, performs at the Oaks Theater in Oakmont on Thursday, July 2, 2026, just across the Allegheny River from his hometown.

sources.title: Comedian and native Pittsburgher Eddie Ifft dishes on childhood, showbusiness, and cow poop

events.title: Pittsburgh Symphony Closes Its Season
events.latest_developments: The Pittsburgh Symphony Orchestra capped its classical season with Aaron Copland's 'Appalachian Spring,' and a Post-Gazette analysis found its attendance rising over the year.
events.summary: The Pittsburgh Symphony Orchestra ended its 2025-26 classical season at Heinz Hall downtown with Aaron Copland's 'Appalachian Spring,' as the orchestra drew larger audiences ahead of its summer film-score concerts.

sources.title: Analysis: The Pittsburgh Symphony closes its season with ‘Appalachian Spring’ and rising attendance

around_teams.title: Steelers Add Cornerback Jamel Dean
around_teams.latest_developments: On Steelers Nation Radio's SNR Drive, Matt Williamson and Wes Uhler broke down the Steelers' addition of cornerback Jamel Dean and how he fits the defense.
around_teams.summary: The Steelers added veteran cornerback Jamel Dean, and SNR Drive hosts Matt Williamson and Wes Uhler assessed where he slots into Pittsburgh's secondary.

sources.title: SNR Drive talks on the addition of Jamel Dean on the Steelers defense

around_teams.title: McCarthy Wants a Super Bowl-Style 3-4 Defense
around_teams.latest_developments: A Post-Gazette piece traced how Steelers coach Mike McCarthy wants to rebuild the 3-4 defense behind his Super Bowl win.
around_teams.summary: Post-Gazette writers explained how Steelers coach Mike McCarthy, drawing on the 3-4 scheme that earned him a Super Bowl, aims to have new coordinator Patrick Graham build a similar front in Pittsburgh.

sources.title: Mike McCarthy won a Super Bowl with a 3-4 defense. He wants to emulate it with the Steelers.

around_teams.title: Skenes Debuts a Giant Sheetz Hot Dog
around_teams.latest_developments: Pirates ace Paul Skenes introduced a supersized hot dog at the Sheetz in Shaler.
around_teams.summary: Pirates pitcher Paul Skenes unveiled a custom supersized hot dog at the Sheetz convenience store in Shaler, the latest local tie-in for the franchise's star ace.

sources.title: Pirates pitcher Paul Skenes introduces a supersized hot dog at Sheetz in Shaler

team_usa.title: Pochettino Reflects as USMNT Nears Türkiye Finale
team_usa.latest_developments: Coach Mauricio Pochettino said he felt 'naïve' about the program's complacency when he took over in 2024, with the United States, already through to the round of 32, set to close Group D against Türkiye on Thursday.
team_usa.summary: Mauricio Pochettino, reflecting on a dominant start to the 2026 World Cup the United States co-hosts, called the team's earlier complacency a 'big punch' and credited his cultural overhaul as the U.S. weighs resting starters in its group finale against Türkiye.

sources.title: Pochettino: USMNT complacency was a 'big punch'
USMNT's World Cup success brings dilemma: Rotate the squad or keep the same XI?

team_usa.title: New York Studies a 2042 Winter Olympics Bid
team_usa.latest_developments: New York Governor Kathy Hochul launched a year-long review of a joint Lake Placid-New York City bid for the 2042 Winter Olympics.
team_usa.summary: Governor Kathy Hochul formed a committee to study whether Lake Placid in the Adirondacks and New York City could jointly host the 2042 Winter Games, citing existing venues and a shift in International Olympic Committee site policy.

sources.title: New York eyes 2042 Winter Olympics with Lake Placid-NYC bid concept

team_usa.title: Bode Miller Arrested on Drug Charges
team_usa.latest_developments: Olympic gold-medal skier Bode Miller pleaded not guilty to two misdemeanor drug charges after his arrest in Idaho for possessing psilocybin mushrooms.
team_usa.summary: Bode Miller, the U.S. Olympic gold medalist alpine skier, was arrested in Idaho on a charge of possessing psilocybin mushrooms and has pleaded not guilty to two misdemeanor counts.

sources.title: Ex-Olympic skier Miller arrested on drug charges

reading.author: Stratechery
reading.title: My Vibe Coding Adventure, The App and the Experience, Ten Takeaways
reading.summary: Ben Thompson recounts using AI tools to 'vibe code' an app he plans to use regularly, walking through the finished product, the building experience, and ten broader takeaways about what AI-assisted software development means.

reading.author: Ed Zitron
reading.title: Cargo Culture
reading.summary: Zitron argues the tech and AI industry imitates the outward rituals and trappings of past successes while lacking the underlying substance and economics that made them work, a cargo-cult mindset he traces through companies like Nvidia and Anthropic.

reading.author: Cal Newport
reading.title: Dear AI Companies: Stop the “Doom Trolling”
reading.summary: Newport contends AI firms undercut their own credibility by publicizing apocalyptic warnings about their products as a marketing tactic, illustrating the absurdity with a hypothetical of Ford warning that its F-150 could endanger humanity.

sports_media: Not Just Football with Cam Heyward
